channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Lockheed Martin Information Session Moved to Virtual
item.description: The Lockheed Martin Information Session scheduled for Tuesday, March 10, 2026, from 5:00–6:30 PM has been moved to a virtual format. The registration link and the link to join the session are available on the event page in Handshake: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1897841/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Spring 2026 School of Engineering Midterm Student Course Evaluations Now Open
item.description: Date: Wednesday, February 25, 2026 (all day) - Spring 2026 School of Engineering (SoE) Midterm course evaluations are now open. Your feedback is very important to the Institute and the school, so please complete the SoE midterm evaluation surveys for all your courses! Evaluations will be available through Friday, March 13, 2026.
item.author: Students

item.title: Personal Statement Workshop for Law School Applicants
item.description: Students who are currently in Pre-Law and those interested in the program, are invited to attend the Personal Statement Workshop for Law School Applicants on March 11, from 4 – 5:30 at Sage Labs, room 4304. There, you will get to work on your personal statement with support from the HASS Hub and Student Success Writing Lab.
item.author: Students

item.title: Meet Morgan Stanley Technology (Virtual Information Session)
item.description: This session is for RPI students interested in pursuing a summer internship or full-time opportunity in financial services technology. Students will meet with business representatives from the firm to gain valuable career advice and mentorship. Register on Handshake here: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1920821/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Grad School Preparation & Accelerated Masters Info Session
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 6:00 PM - 7:00 PM - Join our online Grad School Preparation & Accelerated Master’s Info Session, where we will host an open Q&A and provide a brief overview of the admissions process. This interactive session is designed to give you the opportunity to ask questions for our panel and gain firsthand insight into graduate study at RPI.
item.author: Students

item.title: Computer Science Thesis Defense
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 12, 2026 10:00 AM - 12:00 PM - Name: Qitong Wang Advisor: Prof. Mohammed J. Zaki Day: March 12 (Thursday) Time: 10:00 am Location: Sage 4112 https://rensselaer.webex.com/meet/wangq19
item.author: Students

item.title: From Troy to Cambridge: Jayashree Balaraman Makes RPI History
item.description: Jayashree Balaraman has just become the first RPI student ever to win a Gates Cambridge Scholarship — one of the most prestigious graduate fellowships in the world, awarded to exceptional students from outside the United Kingdom to pursue postgraduate study at the University of Cambridge. She will head to England this fall to begin a Ph.D. program in Pharmacology.
item.author: Community

item.title: Math Colloquium: Dr. Shukai Du from Syracuse University
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 11, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “Accelerating finite element methods via machine learning on a single element” by Dr. Shukai Du. He is an assistant professor at Syracuse University, and his research interests include Numerical analysis, scientific machine learning, and inverse problems.
item.author: Community

item.title: Success Center Tutors Hosting Review Sessions
item.description: Cal II: Tuesday, 3/10 7-9pm in the Nason Classroom ENGR Dynamics: Thursday, 3/12 7-9pm at the Success Center
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - PhishStick Security Analyst
item.description: Our Information Security team has deployed a Security Analyst tool as part of our AI Phishing Coach: PhishStick. PhishStick will assist with protecting our RPI Community from advanced email-based Phishing attacks. Please click above link for more information regarding reporting phishing messages to Microsoft, and features of our PhishStick Security Analyst.
item.author: Community

item.title: Accepted Student Celebrations – Undergraduate Admissions
item.description: The Office of Undergraduate Admissions will host two Accepted Student Celebrations this spring - Saturday, March 21 and Saturday, April 11. We look forward to celebrating with admitted students and their families and highlighting how an RPI education will prepare them to change the world. If you or a campus organization you are part of would like to volunteer during the event, please contact Arianna Livshits, Senior Event Planner, at paluma3@rpi.edu.
item.author: Community

item.title: Hamnet
item.description: Tuesday / March 10, 2026 at 7:00 pm From Academy Award® winning writer/director Chloé Zhao, Hamnet tells the powerful story of love and loss that inspired the creation of Shakespeare’s timeless masterpiece, Hamlet. Presented by UPAC Film/Video Directed by Chloé Zhao
item.author: Community

item.title: 03/11/2026 Onboarding session for Alorithmiq’s Tensor-Network Error Mitigation used with Quantum
item.description: RPI has licensed Alorithmiq’s Tensor-Network Error Mitigation (TEM) Qiskit function for use with our dedicated IBM Quantum System One and Premium IBM Quantum Cloud account. An onboarding session is scheduled for Wednesday March 11th, 9:30 AM in CII 3206. Please click in for additional details.
item.author: Community

item.title: Outlook - Manage time zone settings to avoid meetings and Bookings issues
item.description: Having different time zone settings between individuals in a meeting invite or using Microsoft Bookings can cause issues or confusion on the actual time of the event. For Microsoft Bookings availability is based on the time zone of the person booking which can cause confusion. Click in for link to article.
item.author: Community

item.title: Peer Career Advisor Applications Open Now!
item.description: The CCPD is now accepting applications for our Peer Career Advisor program for the 2026-2027 academic year! Student Qualifications: Must be a sophomore, junior, senior, or graduate student at the start of 2026-2027 academic year! Availability for occasional evening or weekend hours Students in Good Academic & Disciplinary Standing All additional student qualifications are in the application. Student Benefits: Earn $16 per hour! Gain experience developing personal career preparation skills Learn…
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Workshop- Book Discussion: “The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People: Powerful Lessons in Personal Change”-Virtual
item.description: Join an instructor-led discussion of Stephen R. Covey’s incredibly successful book (available in Percipio). It offers a revolutionary program to breaking the patterns of self-defeating behavior that keep us from achieving our goals and reaching our fullest potential and describes how to replace them with a principle-focused approach to problem-solving.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Rensselaer Faulty and Staff In Person Workshop: Active Learning Examples to Keep Your Students Engaged
item.description: Date: Monday, March 16, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - If you are interested in hearing more about various active learning exercises that you may be able to implement into your course, then this workshop is for you. Please join me to discuss the practice of active learning, the benefits, and some of the more popular active learning exercises. Maureen Fodera (Office of Undergraduate Education) will be presenting this webinar. Please register by 3/11 using the Event URL in the See section of the…
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “Leading Hybrid Teams That Actually Work”
item.description:
Hybrid work is no longer a policy decision—it’s a leadership capability. Many leaders are managing hybrid teams using outdated assumptions from either fully in-office or fully remote environments, resulting in friction, slow decisions, and uneven performance. This Leadercamp helps leaders shift from generic hybrid rules to intentional practices that build clarity, trust, and results. Two times: 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM and 12:00 PM- 1:00 PM.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
